name: git_operations
description: Run common git operations in a project directory. Supports status, branch, checkout, add, commit, add-commit (combined stage+commit), diff, log, config, stash, and show (view committed files). Automatically configures git identity on commit if not set.
when_to_use: Use for all git operations instead of raw git commands. The commit action auto-configures git identity (avoiding the common 'Author identity unknown' error that wastes a round trip). The show action lets you view the original committed version of a file without stashing. The add-commit action combines staging all changes and committing in one step, useful for checkpoints during development.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Git project (e.g., /app)

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action to perform: status, branch, checkout, add, commit, add-commit, diff, log, config, stash, show

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Additional arguments depending on the action. For add-commit: <message> (stages all and commits). For show: <ref:path> (e.g., HEAD:path/to/file). For add: <files...> (comma or space separated).

examples.expected: Shows git status of the project.
Commits staged changes with auto-configured git identity.

examples.call: main.sh /app add-commit 'Add module cache with symlink resolution'
examples.expected: Stages all changes and commits in one step with auto-configured git identity.
Shows the original committed version of functions.go with line numbers.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate git commands (cd to project, git command, possibly fixing identity issues) with a single script call. The auto-configure feature prevents the 'Author identity unknown' error that costs a round trip to fix. The show action replaces the save-test-restore workflow (stash, test, stash pop) with a single non-destructive call, saving 2 tool calls. The add-commit action combines staging all changes and committing into one step, saving an additional tool call compared to separate add and commit commands.
